name: Bean Sprout Salad with Garlic
recipeInstructions:
Makes 4 to 6 Servings
Fresh bean sprouts are a must for this recipe from China's Hunan province. The canned variety don't have the requisite crispness. Keep a close eye on the toasting sesame seeds so they don't burn.
1. Preheat oven to 300°F.
2. Toast the sesame seeds in a pie tin, spreading them evenly and roasting for 12-to-16 minutes, stirring often, until golden. Store toasted seeds in an airtight container if toasting ahead of time.
3. Place bean sprouts in a large heatproof bowl.
4. In a medium skillet over moderately low heat, stir-fry garlic, scallions, and ginger in oil for 2-3 minutes until limp.
5. Add remaining ingredients; increase heat to moderate. Bring mixture to boil uncovered for 1 minute to slightly reduce liquid.
6. Pour boiling dressing over bean sprouts; toss well. Cover bowl and chill salad several hours. Toss again before serving.
